text.data-narration: Agile is a software development methodology that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and rapid delivery

g.text: Iterative Development: Break down the development process into short cycles (iterations) that focus on a specific set of requirements.
Flexibility: Allow for changes in requirements and adapt to new information throughout the development process.
Collaboration: Involve team members, customers, and stakeholders in the development process to ensure everyone is aligned and informed.
Continuous Improvement: Regularly reflect on the development process and make improvements to increase efficiency and effectiveness.

text.data-narration: Extreme Programming (XP) is a software development methodology that emphasizes technical practices to improve the quality and speed of software development

g.text: Pair Programming: Two developers work together on the same code, sharing a single workstation to improve code quality and reduce errors.
Continuous Integration: Regularly integrate code changes into a central repository to ensure that the entire development team has access to the latest code.
Refactoring: Regularly review and improve the code to increase maintainability and readability.

text.data-narration: Agile methodologies, such as Scrum and Kanban, emphasize collaboration, flexibility, and continuous improvement to deliver high-quality software quickly and efficiently
